title: Full Stack Software Engineer
jd_text:
Paysol Technologies — Full Stack Software Engineer
Location: London, England, United Kingdom (Remote)
Sector: payments infrastructure

Paysol Technologies is hiring a Full Stack Software Engineer to work on our real-time payments API layer. You will build and maintain services in Node.js and TypeScript, integrating with card networks and banking APIs.

Our stack: Node.js, TypeScript, PostgreSQL, Kafka, Redis, Docker. React on the frontend.

What you need:
- Node.js and TypeScript (3+ years production experience)
- PostgreSQL — schema design and query optimisation
- REST API design and third-party integrations
- Comfortable in a fast-moving fintech environment

What we offer:
Salary: £85,000 - £95,000 per annum + equity + 25 days holiday + private health
- Flexible remote working
- Private health insurance
- Professional development budget (£1,500/yr)

Paysol Technologies is an equal opportunity employer.
reasoning: loc: 'London, England, United Kingdom (Remote)' → London or Remote found → LONDON_OR_REMOTE. role: 'Full Stack Software Engineer' → no senior keyword, 'Full Stack'/'Mid-Level'/'Software Engineer II'/'Fullstack' keyword → MID_LEVEL. tech: Node.js and TypeScript found as required skills → NODE_JS_TS. comp: '£85,000 - £95,000' → GBP midpoint £90k → RANGE_75_99K.
label: good_fit

title: Software Engineer II
jd_text:
ShopStream Analytics — Software Engineer II
Location: London, England, United Kingdom (Hybrid)
Sector: e-commerce analytics

ShopStream Analytics is looking for a Software Engineer II to build data pipeline services for our e-commerce analytics product. You'll write high-throughput ingest services in Node.js and TypeScript connecting retail partners to our analytics engine.

Stack: Node.js, TypeScript, AWS (Lambda, SQS, DynamoDB), Elasticsearch.

Required:
- Node.js and TypeScript (2+ years)
- AWS cloud services experience
- Event-driven architecture patterns
- Good instincts for performance and reliability

What we offer:
Compensation: £85,000 - £95,000 per year + annual bonus + share options
- Flexible remote working
- Private health insurance
- Professional development budget (£1,500/yr)

ShopStream Analytics is an equal opportunity employer.

title: Full Stack Engineer
jd_text:
DataNorth — Full Stack Engineer
Location: United Kingdom (Remote)
Sector: data analytics platforms

DataNorth is hiring a Full Stack Engineer to build our API layer and streaming data services. You'll work in Node.js and TypeScript daily, creating GraphQL and REST endpoints for our analytics product.

Stack: Node.js, TypeScript, GraphQL, PostgreSQL, GCP.

You'll need:
- Node.js and TypeScript — 2+ years production, you should know streams and async patterns
- GraphQL API design
- PostgreSQL — query optimisation and migrations
- GCP (Cloud Run, Pub/Sub, BigQuery)

What we offer:
Package: £85,000 - £95,000 base + learning budget + enhanced pension
- Flexible remote working
- Private health insurance
- Professional development budget (£1,500/yr)

DataNorth is an equal opportunity employer.

title: Mid-Level Backend Developer
jd_text:
GreenOps — Mid-Level Backend Developer
Location: Remote, United Kingdom
Sector: sustainability and carbon accounting software

GreenOps is growing its engineering team. We need a Mid-Level Backend Developer to build our carbon calculation APIs in Node.js and TypeScript. Our backend is fully Node.js/TypeScript, with PostgreSQL and TimescaleDB for time-series emissions data.

Required skills:
- Node.js and TypeScript (2+ years)
- PostgreSQL and TimescaleDB
- REST API design
- Nice to have: Python for ML model integration, Terraform

What we offer:
Salary: £85,000 - £95,000 depending on experience + EMI options + 30 days holiday
- Flexible remote working
- Private health insurance
- Professional development budget (£1,500/yr)

GreenOps is an equal opportunity employer.

title: Fullstack Software Engineer
jd_text:
Claridge Workplace — Fullstack Software Engineer
Location: London, England, United Kingdom (Hybrid)
Sector: B2B workplace management SaaS

Claridge Workplace is rebuilding its integration layer and needs a Fullstack Software Engineer. You'll design and ship REST and webhook services using Node.js and TypeScript, connecting our SaaS platform to HR systems, calendaring tools, and access control APIs.

Stack: Node.js, TypeScript, Prisma, RabbitMQ, PostgreSQL, Docker.

Experience needed:
- Node.js and TypeScript (2+ years production)
- PostgreSQL and ORM experience (Prisma a plus)
- Event-driven messaging (RabbitMQ or similar)
- Docker and containerised deployments

What we offer:
Total compensation: £85,000 - £95,000 base + pension (8% employer) + 25 days holiday
- Flexible remote working
- Private health insurance
- Professional development budget (£1,500/yr)

Claridge Workplace is an equal opportunity employer.
reasoning: loc: 'London, England, United Kingdom (Hybrid)' → London or Remote found → LONDON_OR_REMOTE. role: 'Fullstack Software Engineer' → no senior keyword, 'Full Stack'/'Mid-Level'/'Software Engineer II'/'Fullstack' keyword → MID_LEVEL. tech: Node.js and TypeScript found as required skills → NODE_JS_TS. comp: '£85,000 - £95,000' → GBP midpoint £90k → RANGE_75_99K.

title: Senior Backend Engineer
jd_text:
Habr — Senior Backend Engineer
Location: Remote (UK)
Sector: property technology

About Habr:
We're a fast-growing property technology company based in the UK, building products used by hundreds of thousands of customers.

Role Overview:
• Design and build REST and GraphQL APIs serving 2M+ monthly active users
• Lead technical design sessions and review code across the backend team
• Own the reliability of core property search and listing services
• Collaborate with product managers to define and ship new features

What you'll need:
• 5+ years backend engineering, at least 2 in Node.js
• Strong TypeScript (strict mode) — we use it everywhere
• Experience with PostgreSQL and Redis at scale
• Comfortable with AWS (Lambda, SQS, RDS, CloudFront)

Compensation:
• £75,000 - £90,000 per annum
• 25 days holiday + bank holidays
• Private health insurance
• £1,500 annual learning budget
• Stock options

Habr is committed to building a diverse and inclusive team.
reasoning: loc: 'London, England, United Kingdom (Remote)' → London or Remote found → LONDON_OR_REMOTE. role: 'Senior Backend Engineer' → 'Senior' keyword → SENIOR_PLUS. tech: Node.js and TypeScript found as required → NODE_JS_TS. comp: '£75,000 - £90,000' → GBP range midpoint ~£84k-87k → RANGE_75_99K.
label: good_fit

title: Senior Backend Engineer
jd_text: Habr is a property technology company based in the UK. We are looking for a Senior Backend Engineer to join our growing engineering team at Remote (UK). In this role you will be responsible for design and build rest and graphql apis serving 2m+ monthly active users, as well as lead technical design sessions and review code across the backend team and own the reliability of core property search and listing services. You will also collaborate with product managers to define and ship new features. To succeed in this role, you will need 5+ years backend engineering, at least 2 in node.js alongside strong typescript (strict mode) — we use it everywhere. We also require experience with postgresql and redis at scale and comfortable with aws (lambda, sqs, rds, cloudfront). We offer a salary of £75,000 - £90,000 per annum, 25 days annual leave, private medical cover, and a learning and development budget. We are an equal opportunity employer and welcome applicants of all backgrounds. Experience with Node.js, TypeScript, PostgreSQL, Redis, AWS is essential for this position.
reasoning: loc: 'London, England, United Kingdom (Remote)' → London or Remote found → LONDON_OR_REMOTE. role: 'Senior Backend Engineer' → 'Senior' keyword → SENIOR_PLUS. tech: Node.js and TypeScript found as required → NODE_JS_TS. comp: '£75,000 - £90,000' → GBP range midpoint ~£84k-87k → RANGE_75_99K.
label: good_fit

title: Senior Backend Engineer
jd_text:
HABR — SENIOR BACKEND ENGINEER
LOCATION: REMOTE (UK)

COMPANY OVERVIEW
Habr is a growing property technology business serving customers across the UK.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
- Design and build REST and GraphQL APIs serving 2M+ monthly active users
- Lead technical design sessions and review code across the backend team
- Own the reliability of core property search and listing services
- Collaborate with product managers to define and ship new features

REQUIRED SKILLS
- 5+ years backend engineering, at least 2 in Node.js
- Strong TypeScript (strict mode) — we use it everywhere
- Experience with PostgreSQL and Redis at scale
- Comfortable with AWS (Lambda, SQS, RDS, CloudFront)

WHAT WE OFFER
- SALARY: £75,000 - £90,000 PER ANNUM
- 25 DAYS ANNUAL LEAVE
- PRIVATE HEALTH INSURANCE
- LEARNING & DEVELOPMENT BUDGET

title: Senior Node.js Engineer — Platform Migration
jd_text:
Orbital Commerce is migrating our core platform away from Node.js towards Python (FastAPI + Celery). We are looking for a Senior Engineer with Node.js experience to lead this migration project over the next 12 months.

The ideal candidate understands Node.js deeply enough to read and document our legacy codebase, then port services to Python. Familiarity with Node.js is a hard requirement for reading the existing code — however, all new services will be written in Python.

Responsibilities:
- Lead the incremental migration from Node.js to Python services
- Document and analyse existing Node.js services before porting
- Write Python microservices to replace each Node.js module
- Mentor junior engineers on Python best practices

Salary: £75,000 - £90,000. Remote-first, London office available.

Finbourne Technology is rebuilding our investment data platform. Our existing backend is written in Node.js and we are gradually migrating it to Go. We need a Senior Developer to help drive this migration.

- Node.js — must be able to read and reason about production Node.js code
